title: Observability Fast-Fail (provider sub-stage on fast-fail)
description: When the AI provider exits via a fast-fail path (e.g. provider_unavailable) emit the provider sub-stage with status=failed + failureReason + retryable + duration, so pipeline traces stay structurally complete even when the gateway cannot execute. Purely observational; no retry/pricing/worker behavior change.

verification.summary: Rebased onto post-PR#8 main (728b9ac) and re-verified in isolation: tsc 0 errors; eslint 4 files clean; test:ai 121/121 (incl. 4 OBS-002); ai-regression 2/2; full suite 466/466 (book-now/recovery incl.); next build ✓ (133 pages); legacy schema-compat + off-trace no-op tests pass. GitHub Vercel + regression green on merge commit da30b85. Live Preview gateway (successful-provider) run still deferred — Preview AI Gateway creds unavailable.

knownDifferences: Downstream junk-analysis had no provider markStage wiring (OBS-001 port gap); OBS-002 adds the observability import + fail-branch marking only (fast-fail scope). Success-path provider marking remains a separate OBS-001 completeness follow-up.

rollbackSteps: Rollback: git revert -m 1 da30b85cf3e4d5db5a9e35a1afdd01f924886dce (the PR #7 merge). markStageFailure no-ops when no pipeline trace is active (AI_PIPELINE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED off) — inert by default.
Revert the downstream sync PR. markStageFailure no-ops when no pipeline trace is active (AI_PIPELINE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED off) — inert by default.
No migration, no env, no flag change.
compatibilityNotes: Depends on OBS-001 (observability layer) — present downstream.
ADAPTATION: supercharged's junk-analysis.ts has no observability import and does not mark the provider stage; the port adds the import + the fail-branch markStageFailure call (fast-fail scope only).
Backward compatible: StageStat gains OPTIONAL status/failureReason/retryable; failure result gains OPTIONAL latencyMs/retryable; legacy trace records unaffected.
No new feature flag; inert unless AI_PIPELINE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED is on (a trace must be active).
